response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong full-stack engineer with solid software craftsmanship practices, but he does not meet the core mandatory requirements for this role. His C++ exposure amounts to roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project — far short of the 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development required. His stack is overwhelmingly TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. He has no demonstrable fixed income, derivatives, or capital markets domain knowledge, and no listed experience with SQL Server or Oracle. A brief fintech stint at RIDE Capital involved Node/GraphQL, not C++ or financial instruments.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship culture: TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD, and pairing practiced consistently across multiple roles
Fintech-adjacent experience at RIDE Capital demonstrates some exposure to investment and financial services contexts
Polyglot background with Java, scripting, and CI/CD pipeline experience partially satisfying optional desirables
concerns: C++ professional experience is approximately 6 months (National Instruments, 2014–2015) versus the 5–10 years mandated — a critical gap
Zero fixed income, derivatives, investment banking, or capital markets domain knowledge anywhere in the resume
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database work is primarily MongoDB and MySQL, which do not map to the mandatory SQL requirement
